steps.title: Identify the grammatical requirement
steps.content: The sentence structure is 'spent over fourteen years [blank] data.' The key is that 'spent' + time amount + verb form creates a pattern where the verb must show the activity.

steps.title: Apply the 'spent time [doing]' rule
steps.content: The correct rule is: use the gerund form after 'spent [time]' to show the activity. 'Collecting' is the gerund that completes 'spent years collecting data.' A uses present tense rather than gerund; C uses plural 'were' when the subject is singular; D uses simple past, which doesn't create the correct pattern. That matches choice B — B is correct.
